title: Add grave number editing and grave deletion from admin panel

requirements.summary: Add the ability to edit a grave's plot number and alley directly from the admin panel in both the tile map view and grave management list, persisting changes to the backend
acceptanceCriteria: Admin can open an edit dialog/form for any existing grave from both the map tile view and the list view.
The edit form includes a field to change the plot number (and alley if needed).
Saving the form updates the grave record in the backend.
The updated plot number is immediately reflected in the map and list views after saving.
Validation prevents saving an empty or duplicate plot number within the same alley.
Backend exposes an updateGrave (or equivalent) function that accepts a modified GraveRecord and persists the changes.

file_operations.description: Extend the existing edit dialog to support editing plot number and alley fields for existing graves. Add validation to prevent empty or duplicate plot numbers within the same alley. Wire up the existing updateGrave backend method (already available in backend interface) to persist changes. Ensure the dialog can be opened from both AdminGraveTileMap and GraveManagement components. Add client-side validation and error handling for duplicate plot numbers.

Add or verify a mutation hook for updateGrave that calls actor.updateGrave(id, updatedRecord) and invalidates relevant query caches (cemetery state, all graves, paginated graves, public tiles). Include error handling for validation errors returned from the backend and display toast notifications on success/failure.

Ensure the tile click handler opens GraveEditDialog with the selected grave data, allowing the admin to edit plot number and alley. Verify that the dialog is properly wired and that changes trigger a re-fetch of cemetery data to update the map display immediately.

Ensure each grave entry in the management list can open GraveEditDialog for editing. Verify that the dialog supports editing plot number and alley, and that successful updates trigger cache invalidation to refresh the list view immediately.

requirements.summary: Add the ability to delete a grave record directly from the admin panel in both the tile map view and grave management list, with mandatory confirmation dialog before deletion
acceptanceCriteria: Admin can trigger a delete action for any grave from both the map tile view and the list view.
A confirmation dialog appears asking the admin to confirm deletion before proceeding.
Confirming deletion removes the grave record from the backend.
The deleted grave tile/entry disappears from the map and list views immediately after deletion.
Cancelling the confirmation dialog leaves the grave record unchanged.
Backend exposes a deleteGrave (or equivalent) function that removes a grave by its identifier.

file_operations.description: Add a delete button to the grave edit dialog that triggers a confirmation dialog before calling the backend removeGrave method. Include proper error handling and success feedback. Ensure the dialog closes after successful deletion and cache invalidation occurs.

Add or verify a mutation hook for removeGrave that calls actor.removeGrave(id) and invalidates relevant query caches (cemetery state, all graves, paginated graves, public tiles, grave statistics). Include error handling and display toast notifications on success/failure of deletion.

Wire the delete functionality into the grave edit workflow when triggered from the tile map. Ensure that after successful deletion, the cemetery map re-fetches and updates to remove the deleted grave tile immediately.

Wire the delete functionality into the grave management list. Ensure that after successful deletion via the edit dialog, the list view re-fetches and updates to remove the deleted grave entry immediately.
